goal: Implement the New Bark Town starting area cluster: player bedroom (2F), house ground floor (1F), town exterior, and Elm's Lab — establishing the game's initial spawn, opening cutscene, starter selection, and first story flags.
abstract_tasks: Migrate new_bark_town exterior (20x18 tiles, TILESET_JOHTO) to new canonical layout: walkable paths, house footprints for Elm's Lab, player's house, neighbor's house, Elm's house, east water border, 4 warps, 4 signs, coord_event teacher-blocks at y=8/y=9, Silver rival object

Migrate players_house_2f (8x6 tiles, TILESET_PLAYERS_ROOM): player spawn at (3,3), staircase warp to 1F at (7,0), PC/radio/bookshelf BG events, decoration object events (console, doll_1, doll_2, big_doll), MAPCALLBACK_TILES for decoration toggle

Migrate players_house_1f (10x8 tiles, TILESET_PLAYERS_HOUSE): staircase warp to 2F at (9,0), two exit door warps to New Bark Town, Mom NPC with three time-of-day variants (morning/day/night positions), neighbor NPC, BG events for stove/sink/fridge/TV, coord_event MeetMom scene at (8,4)/(9,4), Pokegear gift

Migrate elms_lab (10x12 tiles, TILESET_LAB): healing machine at (2,1), 4 bookshelves, 4 travel-tip books, PC, trash can, window BG event, 3 starter Poke Ball objects (Cyndaquil at 6,3 / Totodile at 7,3 / Chikorita at 8,3), Elm NPC with dynamic position callback, Aide NPC, Officer Jenny NPC (conditional)

Implement coord_event scene system for Elm's Lab: SCENE_ELMSLAB_CANT_LEAVE at y=6, SCENE_ELMSLAB_MEET_OFFICER at y=5, SCENE_ELMSLAB_AIDE_GIVES_POTION and SCENE_ELMSLAB_AIDE_GIVES_POKE_BALLS both at y=8

Implement story event flags and items: ENGINE_POKEGEAR + Mom phone registration, starter gift (Lv5 holding Berry) with EVENT_GOT_{CHIKORITA/CYNDAQUIL/TOTODILE}_FROM_ELM, Potion on first exit, Poke Ball x5 on egg return, Everstone post-Togepi, Master Ball post-8-badges, S.S. Ticket post-E4

Register shared-template buildings referenced from New Bark Town: players_neighbors_house and elms_house as GenericHouse templates with correct warp_ids, so New Bark Town warps 2 and 3 resolve without missing target errors

Run headless simulation: verify player spawns at players_house_2f (3,3), descends to 1F, MeetMom scene fires Pokegear gift, exits to New Bark Town, enters Elm's Lab, starter selection triggers correctly, teacher blocks western exit before starter obtained

source_data.area_data_summary: new_bark_town: 20x18 tiles, TILESET_JOHTO, MUSIC_NEW_BARK_TOWN, no pokecenter/mart, connections west to route_29 (offset 0) and east to route_27 (Surf required), 4 warps (elms_lab at 6,3; players_house_1f at 13,5; players_neighbors_house at 3,11; elms_house at 11,13), 3 NPCs (teacher spin-random at 6,8; fisher walk-up-down at 12,9; rival Silver facing-right at 3,2 conditional), coord_events at (1,8) and (1,9) for teacher-blocks-exit, initial spawn at players_house_2f (3,3) and town respawn at (13,6). players_house_2f: 8x6 tiles, TILESET_PLAYERS_ROOM, single stair warp at (7,0) to players_house_1f warp_id 3, spawn at (3,3), BG events: PC (2,1), Radio (3,1), Bookshelf (5,1), Poster (6,0 conditional), object_events: console (4,2), doll_1 (4,4), doll_2 (5,4), big_doll (0,1), MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP runs ToggleDecorationsVisibility, MAPCALLBACK_TILES runs ToggleMaptileDecorations. players_house_1f: 10x8 tiles, TILESET_PLAYERS_HOUSE, 3 warps (exit left 6,7; exit right 7,7 both to new_bark_town warp_id 2; stairs up 9,0 to players_house_2f warp_id 1), 4 NPC variants of Mom (main at 7,4 day; morning at 2,2; night at 0,2) plus neighbor at 4,4, BG events stove (0,1) sink (1,1) fridge (2,1) TV (4,1), coord_events MeetMom at (8,4) and (9,4). elms_lab: 10x12 tiles, TILESET_LAB, MUSIC_PROF_ELM, 2 exit warps to new_bark_town warp_id 1 at (4,11) and (5,11), healing machine at (2,1), 4 bookshelves upper row, 4 travel-tip books at (0-3,7), trash (9,3), window (5,0), PC (3,5), Elm NPC at (5,2) moves to (3,4) on init, Aide at (2,9) spin-random, 3 Poke Ball objects at (6,3)/(7,3)/(8,3), Officer Jenny conditional at (5,3), starters: Chikorita/Cyndaquil/Totodile all Lv5 holding Berry, items given conditionally: Potion on first exit, 5 Poke Balls on egg return, Everstone post-Togepi, Master Ball post-8-badges, SS Ticket post-E4, no wild encounters in any area

unstructured_notes: All four areas exist in the old engine and require migration rather than fresh creation. The decoration system in players_house_2f is the most complex part — MAPCALLBACK_TILES must rerender tiles based on EVENT_PLAYERS_HOUSE_2F_CONSOLE, _DOLL_1, _DOLL_2, _BIG_DOLL, and EVENT_PLAYERS_ROOM_POSTER flags. The teacher NPC block is a coord_event scene system: SCENE_NEWBARKTOWN_TEACHER_STOPS_YOU fires at x=1,y=8 and x=1,y=9 and only activates before EVENT_GOT_A_POKEMON_FROM_ELM is set. Elm's Lab MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS moves Elm from (5,2) to (3,4) when SCENE_ELMSLAB_MEET_ELM is active. The rival Silver at (3,2) is conditional on EVENT_RIVAL_NEW_BARK_TOWN — only present before the lab theft. Players_house_2f radio plays MUSIC_POKEMON_TALK (DJ Mary intro) before starter obtained; normal radio afterward. East water of New Bark Town uses FISHGROUP_OCEAN. Elm's lab healing machine uses HEALMACHINE_ELMS_LAB constant and is functional only after starter is chosen. Officer Jenny enters lab and disappears after rival naming sequence (NameRival special + OfficerLeavesMovement).
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

goal: Implement Route 29, the Route 29/46 gate building, and Cherrygrove City — covering the player's first route traversal, one-way ledge mechanics, wild encounters, Guide Gent city tour, rival Silver ambush, PokemonCenter and Mart templates, and Mystic Water gift.
abstract_tasks: Migrate route_29 (60x18 tiles, TILESET_JOHTO, MUSIC_ROUTE_29): connections east to new_bark_town (offset 0), west to cherrygrove_city (offset 0), north gate warp at (27,1) to route_29_route_46_gate; 6 NPC objects including catching-tutorial Dude at (50,12), Youngster at (27,16), Teacher at (15,11), Fisher at (25,3), CooltrainerM night-hint at (13,4), and Tuscany conditional at (29,12) Tuesday-only after Zephyr Badge; no trainers; item ball Potion at (48,2); 2 signs; fruit tree FRUITTREE_ROUTE_29 at (12,2); coord_events for catching tutorial scene at (53,8) and (53,9); time-of-day grass encounters: morning/day Pidgey/Sentret/Rattata/Hoppip Lv2-3, night Hoothoot/Rattata Lv2-3; headbutt TREEMON_SET_ROUTE

Migrate route_29_route_46_gate (8x8 tiles, TILESET_GATE, NorthSouthGate.blk shared layout): north warps to route_46 warp_ids 1 and 2 at (4,0) and (5,0), south warps to route_29 warp_ids 1 and 1 at (4,7) and (5,7); Officer NPC at (0,4) facing right explaining one-way ledge mechanic, Youngster NPC at (6,4) walk-up-down encouraging Route 46 exploration; no trainers or items

Migrate cherrygrove_city exterior (40x18 tiles, TILESET_JOHTO, MUSIC_CHERRYGROVE_CITY): connections north to route_30 (offset 5), east to route_29 (offset 0); 5 warps to cherrygrove_mart (23,3), cherrygrove_pokecenter_1f (29,3), cherrygrove_gym_speech_house (17,7), guide_gents_house (25,9), cherrygrove_evolution_speech_house (31,11); NPCs: Guide Gent at (32,6) conditional EVENT_GUIDE_GENT_VISIBLE_IN_CHERRYGROVE, rival Silver at (39,6) conditional EVENT_RIVAL_CHERRYGROVE_CITY, Teacher at (27,12) walk-left-right, Youngster at (23,7) walk-left-right, Fisher Mystic Water at (7,12); coord_events at (33,6) and (33,7) for SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_MEET_RIVAL; MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP sets ENGINE_FLYPOINT_CHERRYGROVE

Implement Guide Gent city tour event: MUSIC_SHOW_ME_AROUND, scripted movement sequence visiting PokemonCenter->Mart->Route30 exit->sea->house, give Map Card (ENGINE_MAP_CARD), clearevent EVENT_GUIDE_GENT_VISIBLE_IN_CHERRYGROVE, resume map music

Implement rival Silver ambush at Cherrygrove on return from Mr. Pokemon: SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_MEET_RIVAL coord trigger at (33,6/7), rival team selection logic (Totodile starter -> rival uses Chikorita; Chikorita -> Cyndaquil; Cyndaquil -> Totodile), BATTLETYPE_CANLOSE battle, post-battle Silver exits west, SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_NOOP, party healed

Register cherrygrove_pokecenter_1f as shared PokemonCenter template (10x8 tiles, TILESET_POKECENTER): Nurse Joy healer at (3,1), 3 dialogue NPCs, stairs to pokecenter_2f (locked until EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM); register cherrygrove_mart as shared Mart template: phase-1 inventory (no Poke Balls) before Pokedex, phase-2 (adds Poke Balls) after EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM; register guide_gents_house, cherrygrove_gym_speech_house, cherrygrove_evolution_speech_house as GenericHouse templates with their respective NPC dialogues

Implement Mystic Water gift from Fisher NPC at (7,12): verbosegiveitem MYSTIC_WATER on first interaction, EVENT_GOT_MYSTIC_WATER_IN_CHERRYGROVE flag, post-flag dialogue; implement coastal wild encounters: water encounter_rate 6 (Tentacool Lv15-20, Tentacruel Lv20), fishing FISHGROUP_SHORE (Magikarp/Krabby old rod, Corsola day/Staryu night good-rod/super-rod)

Run headless simulation: verify route_29 traversal west from New Bark Town, catching tutorial triggers at (53,8/9), tall grass encounters correct by time-of-day, gate building north exit resolves to route_46, Cherrygrove arrival sets flypoint, Guide Gent tour can be completed and Map Card received, rival battle fires on return trip, Mystic Water obtainable

source_data.area_data_summary: route_29: 60x18 tiles, no trainers, catch-tutorial NPC, Tuscany Tuesday-only gift (Pink Bow after Zephyr Badge), item Potion at (48,2), fruit tree at (12,2), grass encounters morning/day Pidgey/Sentret/Rattata/Hoppip Lv2-3 and night Hoothoot/Rattata, 2 signs at (51,7) and (3,5), ledge strip east-of-center for one-way shortcuts. route_29_route_46_gate: 8x8 tiles NorthSouthGate.blk, 4 warps (north x2 to route_46, south x2 to route_29), Officer at (0,4) explains ledges, Youngster at (6,4) walk-up-down. cherrygrove_city: 40x18 tiles, population 22, connections north route_30 offset 5 / east route_29 offset 0, 5 building warps, Guide Gent at (32,6) gives Map Card via city tour with MUSIC_SHOW_ME_AROUND, rival Silver at (39,6) ambush on return via coord_events at (33,6) and (33,7) using SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_MEET_RIVAL, Teacher at (27,12), Youngster at (23,7), Fisher at (7,12) gives Mystic Water one-time, no trainers, no grass encounters, water encounter_rate 6 (Tentacool/Tentacruel), FISHGROUP_SHORE (Magikarp/Krabby old rod; Corsola day/Staryu night good+super rod), MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP sets ENGINE_FLYPOINT_CHERRYGROVE. cherrygrove_pokecenter_1f: 10x8 shared template, 3 warps (2 exits + stairs to pokecenter_2f), Nurse Joy healer, Fisher + Gentleman + Teacher dialogue NPCs, pokecenter_2f locked until EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM. cherrygrove_mart: 12x8 shared template, MART_CHERRYGROVE (no Poke Balls) -> MART_CHERRYGROVE_DEX (adds Poke Ball) after Pokedex obtained, Clerk shopkeeper, CooltrainerM + Youngster dialogue NPCs. Three generic houses: guide_gents_house (Gramps NPC + magazine bookshelves), cherrygrove_gym_speech_house (Pokefan M explains gyms, Bug Catcher child NPC), cherrygrove_evolution_speech_house (Lass + Youngster explain evolution).

unstructured_notes: Route 29 has NO trainers — intentional since player may not have a starter when first traversing it. The catching tutorial on Route 29 loads a Lv5 Rattata via loadwildmon (not a trainer party), so it must be implemented as a scripted wild encounter rather than a trainer battle. Tuscany appears ONLY on Tuesdays and ONLY after ENGINE_ZEPHYRBADGE is set (post-Violet Gym) — this requires a day-of-week check and badge flag check in the NPC visibility condition. The route_29_route_46_gate north exits go to route_46 which is not built yet — warps must still be registered so the gate does not crash. Rival battle in Cherrygrove is BATTLETYPE_CANLOSE so losing does not result in a game-over. Post-battle party heal is implemented via special HealParty opcode. The mart inventory phase switch is driven by EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM — same flag that unlocks pokecenter_2f stairs and changes the mart CooltrainerM's dialogue. Coral/Staryu time-group fishing uses index 0 in fish.asm (day=Corsola, night=Staryu).

abstract_tasks: Verify player initial spawn at players_house_2f tile (3,3), confirm MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP fires on first visit and initializes events correctly, confirm MAPCALLBACK_TILES applies decoration toggle logic

Verify cherrygrove_pokecenter_1f: Nurse Joy heals party, pokecenter_2f stairs at (0,7) blocked before EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM; cherrygrove_mart: MART_CHERRYGROVE inventory (no Poke Balls) before Pokedex, MART_CHERRYGROVE_DEX inventory after egg delivered; Fisher at (7,12) in Cherrygrove gives Mystic Water once and reverts to fishing dialogue; all generic house warps resolve correctly without crashing

source_data.area_data_summary: QA sprint covering groups 1-2. Key areas under test: players_house_2f (spawn, stairs, decorations), players_house_1f (Mom scenes, stairs, exit warps), new_bark_town (teacher block, rival Silver, Elm's Lab/house warps), elms_lab (heal machine, starter selection, scene progression through CANT_LEAVE->AIDE_GIVES_POTION->MEET_OFFICER->AIDE_GIVES_POKE_BALLS), route_29 (no-trainer traversal, ledges, grass encounters, catching tutorial, fruit tree, item ball), route_29_route_46_gate (ledge tutorial NPC, north/south warps), cherrygrove_city (flypoint, Guide Gent tour with Map Card, rival ambush coord_events, Mystic Water Fisher, connections north/east), cherrygrove_pokecenter_1f (healing, 2F lock), cherrygrove_mart (phase-1 no-balls / phase-2 balls inventory), three generic houses (guide_gents_house, cherrygrove_gym_speech_house, cherrygrove_evolution_speech_house).

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint should audit both the structural correctness (warps resolve, no crashes, flags set) and the behavioral fidelity (scene sequences fire in correct order, rival team selection matches player starter, mart inventory changes at correct trigger, Guide Gent tour is skippable if player says no). The Tuscany NPC on Route 29 (Tuesday / Zephyr Badge) cannot be fully tested until Group 7 is complete, but the NPC object and visibility condition should be verified to not cause a crash on non-Tuesday days. The pokecenter_2f target warp (stairs to Communication Center) can remain as a placeholder target since pokecenter_2f is a shared template built later.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

goal: Implement Route 30 and Mr. Pokemon's House — the first trainer route and story errand destination where the player receives the Mystery Egg from Mr. Pokemon and the Pokedex from Professor Oak.
abstract_tasks: Migrate route_30 (20x54 tiles, TILESET_JOHTO, MUSIC_ROUTE_30, north-south orientation): connections north to route_31 (offset -10), south to cherrygrove_city (offset -5); 2 warps: route_30_berry_house at (7,39) and mr_pokemons_house at (17,5); implement 3 trainers: Bug Catcher Don at (1,7) facing down LOS 3 (Caterpie Lv3 x2), Youngster Mikey at (5,23) facing down LOS 1 (Pidgey Lv2 + Rattata Lv4), Youngster Joey at (2,28) facing right LOS 3 (Rattata Lv4, Pokegear rematch phone, rematch teams JOEY1-JOEY5 via game-progress flags)

Implement Youngster Joey pre-battle cutscene: EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE flag, ROUTE30_MONSTER1 Rattata sprite at (5,24) and ROUTE30_MONSTER2 at (5,25) animate tackle sequence via applymovement, ROUTE30_YOUNGSTER_BATTLE_TRIGGER object at (5,26) initiates actual trainer battle after cutscene clears

Implement route_30 dialogue NPCs: Youngster at (7,30) walk-left-right gives Mr. Pokemon directions (changes text after egg delivered), CooltrainerF at (2,13) warns about eye-contact trainer rule; implement item ball Antidote at (8,35) with EVENT_ROUTE_30_ANTIDOTE, hidden Potion at (14,9) as BG event BGEVENT_ITEM; implement 2 fruit trees FRUITTREE_ROUTE_30_1 at (5,39) and FRUITTREE_ROUTE_30_2 at (11,5); implement 4 signs

Implement route_30 grass wild encounters (encounter_rate 10): morning Ledyba/Caterpie/Pidgey/Weedle/Hoppip Lv3-4, day Pidgey/Caterpie/Pidgey/Weedle/Hoppip Lv3-4, night Spinarak/Hoothoot/Poliwag/Zubat Lv3-4; water encounters (encounter_rate 2) Poliwag/Poliwhirl Lv15-20; FISHGROUP_POND old/good/super rod (Magikarp/Poliwag); headbutt TREEMON_SET_ROUTE; register route_30_berry_house as GenericHouse template warp target

Migrate mr_pokemons_house (8x8 tiles, TILESET_FACILITY, MUSIC_CHERRYGROVE_CITY): 2 exit warps at (2,7) and (3,7) to route_30 warp_id 2 at (17,5); 5 BG events: foreign magazines x2 at (0,1)/(1,1), broken computer x2 at (6,1)/(7,1), strange coins at (6,4); Mr. Pokemon NPC at (3,5) with Mystery Egg gift on first visit and Red Scale -> Exp Share trade in mid-game; Prof. Oak NPC at (6,5) conditional on EVENT_MR_POKEMONS_HOUSE_OAK, gives Pokedex then exits building via scripted movement

Implement SCENE_MRPOKEMONSHOUSE_MEET_MR_POKEMON: sdeferred script sequence — Mr. Pokemon emote, give MYSTERY_EGG, set blackout point to Cherrygrove City, Oak script takes over giving Pokedex (ENGINE_POKEDEX flag), Oak walks to player then exits building (step DOWN LEFT LEFT then disappear), Mr. Pokemon heals party, set CHERRYGROVE_CITY->SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_MEET_RIVAL and ELMS_LAB->SCENE_ELMSLAB_MEET_OFFICER, trigger SPECIALCALL_ROBBED phone-call event

Implement Youngster Joey Pokegear rematch phone system: PHONE_YOUNGSTER_JOEY registration, fight-level advancement tied to ENGINE_FLYPOINT_GOLDENROD (JOEY2), ENGINE_FLYPOINT_OLIVINE (JOEY3), EVENT_CLEARED_RADIO_TOWER (JOEY4), EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR (JOEY5); JOEY5 can give HP_UP item; register route_30_berry_house GenericHouse template so warp at (7,39) resolves without crash

source_data.area_data_summary: route_30: 20x54 tiles (10x27 blocks), TILESET_JOHTO, MUSIC_ROUTE_30, north-south orientation; warps to route_30_berry_house at (7,39) and mr_pokemons_house at (17,5); 3 trainers: Bug Catcher Don at (1,7) LOS 3 (2x Caterpie Lv3), Youngster Mikey at (5,23) LOS 1 (Pidgey Lv2 + Rattata Lv4), Youngster Joey at (2,28) LOS 3 (Rattata Lv4, rematch phone JOEY1-JOEY5); 2 non-trainer NPCs: youngster directions at (7,30) and CooltrainerF at (2,13); 2 item balls: Antidote at (8,35), hidden Potion at (14,9); 2 fruit trees at (5,39) and (11,5); 4 signs; grass encounter_rate 10 (Ledyba/Caterpie/Pidgey/Weedle/Hoppip morning-day, Spinarak/Hoothoot/Poliwag/Zubat night); water encounter_rate 2 (Poliwag/Poliwhirl Lv15-20); FISHGROUP_POND; headbutt TREEMON_SET_ROUTE; pre-battle Rattata cutscene at EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE with MONSTER sprites at (5,24)/(5,25). mr_pokemons_house: 8x8 tiles, TILESET_FACILITY (unusual, collector flavor), MUSIC_CHERRYGROVE_CITY, LANDMARK_ROUTE_30; 2 exit warps to route_30 (17,5); Mr. Pokemon at (3,5) gives MYSTERY_EGG first visit, trades EXP_SHARE for RED_SCALE mid-game; Prof. Oak at (6,5) conditional EVENT_MR_POKEMONS_HOUSE_OAK gives Pokedex then exits; 5 BG events (2x magazines, 2x broken computer, strange coins); no wild encounters; SCENE_MRPOKEMONSHOUSE_MEET_MR_POKEMON sets CHERRYGROVE rival scene and ELMS_LAB officer scene, triggers SPECIALCALL_ROBBED, blackout point Cherrygrove City.

unstructured_notes: Route 30 is a tall north-south route (54 tiles tall vs 18 wide). Joey's Rattata cutscene uses actual sprite objects (ROUTE30_MONSTER1 and MONSTER2) that animate a tackle sequence — these are environment object_events with sprite SPRITE_RATTATA, not wild Pokemon encounters. The ROUTE30_YOUNGSTER_BATTLE_TRIGGER object at (5,26) is a separate event object that fires the trainer battle after the cutscene. Joey's rematch system is the most complex trainer rematch in the early game — he upgrades through 5 fight tiers over the course of the entire game and is the only trainer on Route 30 who gives a phone number. Mr. Pokemon uses TILESET_FACILITY instead of TILESET_HOUSE — this is correct and gives the interior a lab-like feel; do not accidentally apply the house tileset. The blackout point is set to Cherrygrove City (not New Bark Town) during the Mr. Pokemon scene, so if the player faints after this event they respawn in Cherrygrove. Route 30 Berry House at (7,39) should use the GenericHouse shared template — no JSON file exists for it.

goal: Implement Route 31, the Route 31/Violet Gate building, and Violet City exterior — connecting Route 30 to the first gym city with Bug Catcher Wade trainer, Kenya mail sidequest, Dark Cave entrance, and Violet City's full building warp network.
abstract_tasks: Migrate route_31 (40x18 tiles, 20x9 blocks, TILESET_JOHTO): connections south to route_30 (offset 10), east to violet_city (offset -9) via route_31_violet_gate; 3 warps: gate at (4,6) and (4,7) to route_31_violet_gate warp_ids 3 and 4, Dark Cave entrance at (34,5) to dark_cave_violet_entrance warp_id 1; 1 trainer: Bug Catcher Wade at (21,13) facing left LOS 5 (Caterpie Lv2 x3 + Weedle Lv3, Pokegear rematch WADE1-WADE5 via game-progress flags with Metapod/Kakuna then Butterfree/Beedrill teams); implement route_31 grass encounters (encounter_rate 25): morning Caterpie/Ledyba/Pidgey/Kakuna/Bellsprout/Hoppip, day Caterpie/Pidgey/Metapod/Kakuna/Bellsprout/Hoppip, night Rattata/Hoothoot/Poliwag/Spinarak/Gastly/Zubat Lv4-6; headbutt trees Spearow/Exeggcute/Aipom/Hoothoot/Ledyba/Spinarak

Implement Route 31 NPCs: sleepy Fisher (Kenya quest recipient) at (17,7) facing down — accepts Kenya the Spearow holding Elm's Spearow Mail and gives TM50 (Nightmare), event_flag EVENT_GAVE_KENYA / EVENT_GOT_TM50_NIGHTMARE; Youngster at (9,5) walk-around mentions Dark Cave and Falkner; CooltrainerM at (33,8) walk-around wants Flash for Dark Cave; implement 2 item balls: Potion at (29,5) EVENT_ROUTE_31_POTION, Poke Ball at (19,15) EVENT_ROUTE_31_POKE_BALL; fruit tree FRUITTREE_ROUTE_31 at (16,7); 2 signs; MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP runs Route31CheckMomCallCallback (phone call from Mom if not yet spoken to her)

Implement Dark Cave entrance warp at (34,5): must register dark_cave_violet_entrance as a placeholder/future warp target so the engine does not crash when player approaches the cave mouth — the interior is built in a later sprint; register route_31 as a valid Fly connection from violet_city east (offset -9 from attributes.asm)

Migrate route_31_violet_gate (10x8 tiles, east-west gate format, TILESET_GATE): 4 warps — west exit x2 to violet_city warp_ids 8 and 9 at (0,4) and (0,5), east exit x2 to route_31 warp_ids 1 and 2 at (9,4) and (9,5); Officer at (5,2) facing down greeting player and asking about Sprout Tower; CooltrainerF at (1,2) spin-slow lamenting she came too far east; no trainers or items

Migrate violet_city exterior (40x36 tiles, 20x18 blocks, TILESET_JOHTO): connections south to route_32 (offset 0), west to route_36 (offset 0), east to route_31 (offset 9); 9 warps: violet_mart (9,17), violet_gym (18,17), earls_pokemon_academy (30,17), violet_nickname_speech_house (3,15), violet_pokecenter_1f (31,25), violet_kyles_house (21,29), sprout_tower_1f (23,5), route_31_violet_gate upper (39,24) and lower (39,25); implement MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP to set ENGINE_FLYPOINT_VIOLET; implement fruit tree FRUITTREE_VIOLET_CITY at (14,29) PRZCureBerry; 4 item balls: PP Up (4,1) Surf-req, Rare Candy (35,5) Surf-req, hidden Hyper Potion (37,14)

Implement Violet City NPCs: Earl at (13,16) spin-random conditional EVENT_VIOLET_CITY_EARL (checks Zephyr Badge, leads player to Academy via MUSIC_SHOW_ME_AROUND path if beat Falkner); Lass at (28,28) ghosts-in-Sprout-Tower dialogue; Super Nerd at (24,14) gym-prime-time dialogue; Gramps at (17,20) walk-left-right Falkner-inherited-gym dialogue; Youngster at (5,18) spin-random headbutt-tree dialogue; 5 signs (city sign, gym sign, Sprout Tower sign, academy sign, mart/pokecenter signs)

Register Violet City's shared-template and generic house buildings: violet_pokecenter_1f (shared PokemonCenter template, Nurse Joy healer, warp to violet_city warp_id 5), violet_mart (shared Mart template with inventory: Poke Ball/Potion/Escape Rope/Antidote/Paralyze Heal/Awakening/X Defend/X Attack/X Speed/Flower Mail, warp to violet_city warp_id 1), violet_nickname_speech_house (GenericHouse template), violet_kyles_house (GenericHouse template with in-game trade Bellsprout->Onix 'Rocky' Lv10 holding Bitter Berry); Sprout Tower warp at (23,5) to sprout_tower_1f warp_id 1 (sprint 7 area, placeholder now)

Implement Bug Catcher Wade Pokegear rematch system: PHONE_WADE registration, fight-level tiers WADE2 (Metapod x3 + Kakuna Lv9-10, unlock ENGINE_FLYPOINT_GOLDENROD), WADE3 (Butterfree x2 + Beedrill Lv14-15, unlock ENGINE_FLYPOINT_MAHOGANY), WADE4 (Butterfree x2 + Beedrill with moves Lv24-25, unlock EVENT_CLEARED_RADIO_TOWER), WADE5 (Butterfree x2 + Beedrill Lv30-32 with moves, unlock EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR)

source_data.area_data_summary: route_31: 40x18 tiles (20x9 blocks), connections south to route_30 offset 10 / east to violet_city offset -9 via gate; warps: gate at (4,6)+(4,7) to route_31_violet_gate, dark_cave at (34,5); 1 trainer Bug Catcher Wade at (21,13) LOS 5 (Caterpie Lv2x3 + Weedle Lv3, rematch phone WADE1-WADE5); Kenya sidequest Fisher at (17,7) gives TM50 Nightmare; Youngster at (9,5) and CooltrainerM at (33,8) dialogue NPCs; items: Potion (29,5), Poke Ball (19,15), fruit tree (16,7), TM50 quest reward; grass encounter_rate 25 with morning/day Caterpie/Ledyba/Pidgey/Bellsprout/Hoppip and night Rattata/Hoothoot/Poliwag/Spinarak/Gastly/Zubat Lv4-6; headbutt Spearow/Aipom/Exeggcute/Hoothoot/Ledyba/Spinarak; 2 signs. route_31_violet_gate: 10x8 tiles, east-west gate, 4 warps (west x2 to violet_city warp_ids 8+9, east x2 to route_31 warp_ids 1+2), Officer and CooltrainerF NPCs. violet_city: 40x36 tiles (20x18 blocks), 9 warps to all major buildings including sprout_tower_1f, violet_gym, earls_pokemon_academy, pokecenter, mart, kyles_house, nickname_house, and east gate; 5 NPC objects (Earl conditional, Lass, Super Nerd, Gramps, Youngster); items: PP Up Surf-req (4,1), Rare Candy Surf-req (35,5), hidden Hyper Potion (37,14), fruit tree PRZCureBerry (14,29); mart inventory 10 items including X-items and Flower Mail; water encounters (Poliwag/Poliwhirl Lv15-24 via Surf); fishing old/good/super rod Poliwag/Magikarp; MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP sets ENGINE_FLYPOINT_VIOLET; connections south route_32 / west route_36 / east route_31 offset 9.

unstructured_notes: Violet City's south and west connections (route_32 and route_36) are not built in this sprint but must be registered in the map attributes so the engine does not error when loading the city. The Earl outdoor-NPC event is gated by EVENT_VIOLET_CITY_EARL which is set after beating Falkner (Zephyr Badge) — this means the scripted movement leading player to the academy only fires post-gym. Sprout Tower, Violet Gym, and Earl's Academy all have warp entries in this sprint but their interiors are Sprint 7 content — register placeholder warps now. Kyle's House in-game trade (Bellsprout for Onix 'Rocky') is the first in-game trade available and should be at minimum stubbed so the building is enterable without a crash. Bug Catcher Wade's rematch teams have actual move sets from Wade4 onward — the move data must reference valid move IDs. The Dark Cave entrance is a one-way warp (can enter, but interior requires Flash to navigate) — the placeholder target warp must be registered so the warp event tile does not crash.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

goal: QA audit for Groups 4-5: verify Route 30 trainers and Mr. Pokemon's errand sequence, Cherrygrove rival battle, Route 31 traversal with Kenya sidequest, and Violet City's full building warp network and Earl outdoor event.
abstract_tasks: Verify Route 30 trainer battles: Bug Catcher Don at (1,7) initiates battle at LOS 3 (2x Caterpie Lv3); Youngster Mikey at (5,23) initiates at LOS 1 (Pidgey Lv2 + Rattata Lv4); Youngster Joey pre-battle cutscene fires at EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE — MONSTER sprites at (5,24)+(5,25) animate tackle — then trainer battle with Rattata Lv4; all defeated trainers set correct event flags and award prize money

Verify Youngster Joey Pokegear phone registration and rematch system: Joey's phone number added after beating him, rematch scheduling works, JOEY2 team (requires ENGINE_FLYPOINT_GOLDENROD flag) is only available after that city is reached — test that fight level does not advance prematurely

Verify Mr. Pokemon's House first-visit scene: enter from Route 30 warp at (17,5), SCENE_MRPOKEMONSHOUSE_MEET_MR_POKEMON fires, Mr. Pokemon emote and MYSTERY_EGG gift, blackout point set to Cherrygrove City, Prof. Oak appears at (6,5) gives Pokedex (ENGINE_POKEDEX flag), Oak exits building via scripted movement and disappears, Mr. Pokemon heals party, Cherrygrove rival scene and Elm's Lab officer scene flags are set, SPECIALCALL_ROBBED phone event queued

Verify rival Silver ambush on return through Cherrygrove: SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_MEET_RIVAL fires at coord_events (33,6/7) when approaching from Route 30 direction, rival team correctly counters player starter (all three starter choices tested), BATTLETYPE_CANLOSE allows loss, post-battle Silver shoves player south and exits, party healed, SCENE_CHERRYGROVECITY_NOOP set

Verify egg delivery to Elm: return to Elms Lab, SCENE_ELMSLAB_MEET_OFFICER fires, Officer Jenny at (5,3) conditional on EVENT_COP_IN_ELMS_LAB, player names rival via NameRival special, Officer exits via OfficerLeavesMovement, Elm receives Mystery Egg, aide at y=8 coord_event gives 5 Poke Balls (EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM), Cherrygrove mart adds Poke Balls to inventory, Cherrygrove pokecenter 2F stairs unlock

Verify Route 31 traversal: Bug Catcher Wade at (21,13) LOS 5 initiates battle (Caterpie x3 + Weedle), Kenya quest — if Kenya-holding Spearow is in party the Fisher at (17,7) accepts it and gives TM50 Nightmare (EVENT_GOT_TM50_NIGHTMARE); Potion item ball at (29,5) and Poke Ball at (19,15) collectable; fruit tree at (16,7) yields PRZCureBerry daily; Dark Cave entrance at (34,5) can be warped to without crash (placeholder interior)

Verify route_31_violet_gate transitions: east exit from route_31 at (4,6)/(4,7) enters gate at (9,4)/(9,5), west exit from gate at (0,4)/(0,5) enters violet_city at (39,24)/(39,25); Officer NPC and CooltrainerF dialogue; no stuck-warp issues

Verify Violet City exterior: ENGINE_FLYPOINT_VIOLET set on first entry, all 9 building warps resolve correctly (sprout_tower_1f placeholder, violet_gym placeholder, earls_pokemon_academy placeholder, violet_pokecenter healing works, violet_mart inventory correct, kyles_house enterable, nickname_house enterable, east gate bidirectional); Earl NPC at (13,16) only initiates Academy tour after Zephyr Badge obtained; verify fruit tree (14,29) PRZCureBerry; hidden Hyper Potion at (37,14) obtainable

Verify Bug Catcher Wade Pokegear rematch registration and that WADE2 team gates correctly on ENGINE_FLYPOINT_GOLDENROD; run full headless playthrough: Cherrygrove->Route30->Mr.Pokemon->return Cherrygrove rival->New Bark Town->Elm egg delivery->Poke Balls received->Route31->Wade battle->Violet City arrival; flag all test failures for fix

Verify Violet City south connection to route_32 and west connection to route_36 are registered in map attributes (even as placeholder borders) so the city does not error on load — confirm player cannot walk south from Violet City without Zephyr Badge per story gate if implemented, or confirm the badge gate is correctly deferred to Route 32 gatehouse

source_data.area_data_summary: QA sprint covering groups 4-5. Key areas under test: route_30 (Don/Mikey/Joey trainer battles, Joey pre-battle cutscene, item balls, fruit trees, grass encounters, Joey rematch phone), mr_pokemons_house (MYSTERY_EGG gift, OAK Pokedex scene, scene flag chain setting rival+officer scenes, blackout point, Red Scale->Exp Share mid-game trade stub), cherrygrove_city (rival ambush on return, Poke Balls unlocking in mart, pokecenter 2F unlock), elms_lab (Officer Jenny scene, rival naming, Poke Ball x5 gift), route_31 (Wade trainer battle + rematch, Kenya sidequest, item balls, Dark Cave entrance warp, grass encounters), route_31_violet_gate (bidirectional gate warps, NPC dialogues), violet_city (all 9 building warps, flypoint, Earl conditional post-gym tour, item balls Surf-required deferred, fruit tree, signs, mart inventory, pokecenter healing, south/west connection attribute registration).

unstructured_notes: The Kenya sidequest requires the player to have received Kenya the Spearow from Elm (a separate phone-call event that occurs sometime before Route 31 is reached on the return journey). The sidequest delivery is optional and TM50 Nightmare is optional content — verify the Fisher NPC gives the TM when Kenya-holding Spearow is in party, but do not block progression if the player skips it. The Earl Academy tour post-Falkner must be tested in both directions: if player talks to Earl before Falkner is beaten he responds 'Very indeed nice!' and stays put; after Falkner he leads player to the Academy. The Violet City south/west connections (route_32 and route_36) are not playable yet so confirm border transition either gracefully wraps or is blocked — no hard crash allowed.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: All five areas are migration targets — they exist in the old engine and need reconciliation with the new canonical JSON data. The animated Bellsprout pillar in Sprout Tower is a distinctive visual feature; it must sway on all three floors at the same x-column (x=11 in tile coordinates). Earl's broken English dialogue is a Crystal character detail that must be preserved exactly. Elder Li on 3F is implemented as OBJECTTYPE_SCRIPT (not OBJECTTYPE_TRAINER) — his script calls loadtrainer SAGE, LI and startbattle directly; this is unusual and may require special handling. The rival cutscene on 3F uses a coord_event at (11,9) and includes earthquake SFX, NPC movement, and disappear opcode — this is a scene-scripted event that must be implemented as a full scene script. Falkner's post-battle script also sets SCENE_ELMSLAB_NOOP and triggers the Elm aide phone call — these are cross-map flag effects that must fire correctly.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

unstructured_notes: Route 32 is the longest route in the game at 20x90 tiles. The Zephyr Badge gate is a coord_event (not an NPC gate), meaning the engine must support coord_event-triggered story blocks. The Slowpoke Tail salesman uses SCENE_ROUTE32_OFFER_SLOWPOKETAIL with a coord at (7,71) — this scene checks a cross-map event flag from Azalea Town. Frieda of Friday introduces the day-of-week system; her object uses a DISAPPEAR callback driven by VAR_WEEKDAY comparison. The Old Rod is the first fishing rod in the game and is given inside the mid-route Pokemon Center — it must be accessible before the player reaches Azalea Town. Ruins of Alph Outside is the hub for an interconnected sub-dungeon system; the two Union Cave B1F shortcuts at (6,19) and (6,27) create a cross-dungeon connection that must be registered in both maps. The Research Center Unown Dex upgrade uses a complex scripted scene where a scientist walks to a computer and runs equipment SFX before upgrading the Pokedex — this requires the script system to support NPC movement sequences with SFX injection.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

(3) The Ruins of Alph Outside hub has 11 warps connecting 7 different maps — any warp ID mismatch will produce wrong transitions. (4) Earl's flag-based visibility in the Academy uses an inverted event flag (present when flag cleared, absent when set) — confirm this logic is implemented correctly. All encounter tables should be spot-checked against pokecrystal source; use the day table as default until time-of-day is fully implemented.

unstructured_notes: All five chambers are new content with no existing engine implementation. The four puzzle chambers share an identical 8x10 tile layout — a shared template approach would be efficient but each has unique puzzle ID, wall word, target Pokemon, NPC configuration, and flag assignments. The sliding tile puzzle mechanic itself is the most complex new system in this sprint: it requires a puzzle state representation, tile manipulation input, completion detection, and the skyfall transition which is a non-standard warp type (falling through the floor with vertical animation). The floor block conversion (closed floor tiles become hole tiles on puzzle completion) requires MAPCALLBACK_TILES to be called on every map entry to restore the floor if the puzzle was not yet solved in a prior session. The Inner Chamber Unown encounter system is unique: which specific Unown letter forms appear is controlled by a set of ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_* flags, and the selection algorithm in pokecrystal uses a catch-order list to pick which form to show for a given encounter — this is distinct from normal species selection.
technical_debt_notes: This area does not exist in the current engine and is being built fresh. Verify that the map system supports the tile dimensions and features of this new area.

unstructured_notes: Union Cave B2F is the only floor that is entirely new content; 1F and B1F are migration targets from the old engine. The Friday Lapras is one of the most beloved timed events in Pokemon Crystal and must be implemented with a persistent flag so it is truly one-time-per-save-file — not one-time-per-week. The Cool Trainer Nick battle on B2F uses TRAINERTYPE_MOVES (all 3 Kanto starters with custom movesets), which requires the trainer data system to support move-specified teams. The Ruins-of-Alph passages on B1F create a hidden cross-dungeon shortcut; warps (3,3) and (3,11) on B1F must correctly resolve to warps 7 and 8 on ruins_of_alph_outside. Hiker Anthony on Route 33 has the most complex rematch system of any early-game trainer — 5 fight tiers each gated by distinct game-progress flags. Route 33's atmospheric rain weather is a detail that does not affect gameplay in Gen 2 but should be set correctly for immersion.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment (union_cave_1f and union_cave_b1f). Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data. Union Cave B2F is entirely new content — verify that the map system supports its dimensions and special Lapras encounter.

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint covers groups 10 and 11 (sprints 10 and 11). Primary risk areas: (1) The skyfall puzzle transition is a novel warp type — confirm the player lands at the correct Inner Chamber warp position for each of the four chambers and is not placed at a shared warp. (2) The MAPCALLBACK_TILES system must correctly distinguish between pre-solve and post-solve states on every chamber re-entry; failure here means floors would already have holes before the puzzle is done or the reverse. (3) The Unown form selection algorithm depends on catch-order data and unlock flags — test that the correct subset of letters appears before each additional puzzle is solved. (4) The Friday Lapras uses a persistent flag (ENGINE_UNION_CAVE_LAPRAS) not a repeating timer; confirm this is checked before the weekday check so a player who already caught/battled Lapras never sees it again. (5) Cool Trainer Nick's TRAINERTYPE_MOVES team requires all three parties have custom movesets loaded — verify no move data is missing or corrupted. (6) The Ho-Oh Chamber's entry scene calls special HoOhChamber — confirm this special does not crash when Rainbow Wing is not in inventory.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications.

unstructured_notes: Slowpoke Well B1F is the first major Team Rocket confrontation. GRUNTM_1 must be the final trainer defeated — its post-battle script drives the entire story resolution sequence (party heal, warp to Kurt's House, cascade event flags). The 4 Rocket Grunts are all gated under EVENT_SLOWPOKE_WELL_ROCKETS and disappear as a group after resolution. Kurt's House has the most complex NPC state machine of any building seen so far: 5 NPCs each individually toggled based on multiple overlapping event flags. B2F is optional content (Surf required) and yields the only wild Slowbro in the well. The White Apricorn tree in town produces Fast Balls when given to Kurt. Bridget the Lass NPC in Azalea Gym should NOT be confused with the Bridget who cries in Goldenrod Gym — both exist. The rival Silver battle at (5,10)/(5,11) only triggers after Bugsy is defeated and uses SCENE_AZALEATOWN_RIVAL_BATTLE scene control.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

unstructured_notes: Ilex Forest is a CAVE-environment outdoor map — encounters trigger on all walkable tiles, not just tall grass. This is critical: the encounter system must treat it as a cave. The tree layout is a maze; the canonical tile rows are mostly T (tree) fill with sparse walkable paths — exact path from pokecrystal .blk not decomposed here but NPC/item coordinates are exact from pokecrystal source. The Charcoal Kiln Farfetch'd uses SPRITE_MOLTRES in pokecrystal (sprite reuse for walking bird NPC). The TM02 Headbutt given in Ilex Forest is the primary Headbutt acquisition point for the entire game — necessary for tree encounter mechanics throughout Johto. The route_34_ilex_forest_gate is NOT the gate between Ilex and Azalea; it is the northern gate connecting Ilex to Route 34. The Celebi event Teacher2 NPC blocks the south exit from this gate during EVENT_FOREST_IS_RESTLESS — this is Crystal-exclusive content.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

unstructured_notes: Key regression risks from migration: (1) warp ID mismatches between the 4-map gate chain (azalea_town->ilex_azalea_gate->ilex_forest->route34_ilex_gate->route_34) — any coordinate offset error creates soft-locks; (2) EVENT_SLOWPOKE_WELL_ROCKETS visibility flag must hide all 4 Rockets simultaneously after resolution; (3) wFarfetchdPosition RAM variable must survive map transitions during Farfetch'd puzzle; (4) CAVE environment flag on Ilex Forest — if incorrectly set to OUTDOOR, encounters would only trigger on tall_grass tiles instead of all walkable tiles; (5) Hive Badge flag ENGINE_HIVEBADGE must gate Cut field usage, not just obedience.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications.

unstructured_notes: The Day Care Man on Route 34 is a dynamic NPC — he appears outside only when ENGINE_DAY_CARE_MAN_HAS_EGG is set, and the MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS on both Route 34 and Day Care interior must stay in sync. This is one of the first examples of cross-map NPC state management. Officer Keith's night-only restriction requires checktime NITE in his trainer script — during day he gives a patrol dialogue instead of battling. Goldenrod City is the largest map so far; its tile layout is approximately twice the area of any previous city. The Underground has two separate entrances on the city map (north at 9,5 and south at 11,29) that both route through the GOLDENROD_UNDERGROUND_SWITCH_ROOM_ENTRANCES intermediate map before reaching the actual Underground tunnel. The Magnet Train Station warp at (9,13) goes to goldenrod_magnet_train_station but the station is post-game blocked until the player has the Pass.

unstructured_notes: Whitney's Miltank is the most infamous battle in Gen II — Rollout doubles power each turn (base 30->60->120->240->480) and Milk Drink restores 50% HP. The coin system in the Game Corner requires the Coin Case as a key item — the Case is obtained as an item ball in the Underground at (7,25), creating a dependency: Underground must be accessible before Game Corner prizes are usable. The Dept Store elevator requires an intermediate goldenrod_dept_store_elevator map to function — this should be registered as a simple pass-through. B1F is significantly larger than other floors (20x18 vs 16x8) and connects to the Underground Warehouse during the Radio Tower Takeover arc via the Basement Key door. The Haircut Brothers in the Underground boost Pokemon friendship — this is one of the few non-item friendship gain sources in the game. Goldenrod Underground's switch room (GOLDENROD_UNDERGROUND_SWITCH_ROOM_ENTRANCES) is a separate micro-map that handles the entrance door logic and resets switches on every entry.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

unstructured_notes: Key regression risks: (1) Whitney's badge is NEVER given until coord_event fires and EVENT_MADE_WHITNEY_CRY clears — if the cry mechanic is skipped, badge cannot be obtained, soft-locking Strength progression; (2) Coin Case dependency — Game Corner prize vendors must check for the key item before allowing exchange; (3) Underground switch room (separate intermediate map) must reset all switches on EVERY entry to goldenrod_underground — if not reset, switches from a previous session could block passage; (4) Day Care Man NPC state must be synchronized between route_34 and day_care via matching MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS callbacks on both maps; (5) Route 34 Surf area trainers should only activate if player has Surf — test with and without HM.

Critical implementation notes: (1) Radio Tower 5F has the most complex scripted sequence in the game — the Fake Director scene requires a coord_event-triggered battle with a pre-battle NPC movement sequence (Petrel walks LEFT x3 UP x2 before revealing himself); (2) After defeating Archer on 5F, a long post-battle cinematics sequence plays with the Director walking in — this requires a scripted NPC movement from off-screen; (3) The Bug Contest gate uses both MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP and MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS for two different purposes — the newmap callback for mid-contest detection and the objects callback for NPC visibility; (4) Route 35 should have Growlithe as a Crystal-exclusive morning/day wild encounter (not in Gold/Silver).

unstructured_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. This QA covers the most complex scripted event chains in the Goldenrod content block. Priority focus: (1) The Radio Tower takeover event chain is a multi-map story arc — test it end-to-end by simulating the full sequence from takeover trigger through clear; (2) The Fake Director scene on 5F is particularly fragile — verify the scripted NPC movement fires before the battle reveal; (3) The Card Key shutter on 3F must persist its open state across map re-entries (MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP must restore open blocks when EVENT_USED_THE_CARD_KEY_IN_THE_RADIO_TOWER is set); (4) Bug Contest gate dual-callback system must not interfere with normal traversal on non-contest days. Use headless simulation to drive the full Radio Tower clear sequence deterministically.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. unstructured_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. This QA covers a critical story milestone: the player must pass through this region to reach Ecruteak City and earn the Fog Badge, which unlocks Surf and opens up the sea routes to Cianwood City and much of mid-game Johto. Priority focus: (1) The Bug-Catching Contest is a recurring timed event with complex party management — verify it doesn't corrupt the player's party on entry/exit; (2) Sudowoodo removal must be permanent (EVENT_FOUGHT_SUDOWOODO persists in save data); (3) The Ecruteak Gym invisible floor puzzle must have all 33 pit warps correctly placed so stepping on any void tile sends the player back to the re-entry platform — missing even one pit tile could let the player skip the puzzle; (4) Confirm Fog Badge enables the Surf overworld usage gate (required to progress to Cianwood and Routes 40-41); (5) Test that Morty's post-battle auto-beat script correctly marks all 4 sub-trainers as defeated so they don't challenge the player again on re-entry. Use headless simulation to drive through the gym puzzle path deterministically.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

goal: Implement Ecruteak interiors (Dance Theater, Wise Trio's Room, Tin Tower Entrance) and Burned Tower (1F + B1F) — five key story buildings with complex conditional NPC systems and the legendary beast release event
abstract_tasks: Migrate dance_theater: 12x14-tile mansion-tileset building with 5 Kimono Girl trainers (Naoko/Sayo/Zuki/Kuni/Miki, each Lv17 Eeveelution), Gentleman Surf Guy reward (HM03 Surf when all 5 defeated), Rhydon companion NPC, two fancy-panel bg_events, EVENT_GOT_HM03_SURF gate on all-five-defeated check

Migrate wise_trios_room: 8x8-tile minimal building with two-state NPC system (WISE_TRIO_1 observer sages vs WISE_TRIO_2 trainer sages Gaku/Masa/Koji each Lv32 Noctowl+Eeveelution), coord_event blocking east exit during SCENE_WISETRIOSROOM_SAGE_BLOCKS, Koji post-defeat scene granting EVENT_KOJI_ALLOWS_YOU_PASSAGE_TO_TIN_TOWER, third post-Suicune empty state

Migrate tin_tower_entrance: 20x18-tile two-floor gatehouse with WiseTriosRoomWiseTrioCallback, two sage gatekeepers conditionally visible via EVENT_RANG_CLEAR_BELL_1/2, SCENE_ECRUTEAKTINTOWERENTRANCE_SAGE_BLOCKS coord events at (4,7) and (5,7) triggering animated sage-block sequences, multi-state dialogue (no badge / has Fog Badge / post-Radio-Tower-no-bell / has Clear Bell / Koji-allowed)

Migrate burned_tower_1f: 20x18-tile TILESET_RUIN dungeon with BurnedTower1FHoleAndLadderCallback (manages ladder/hole tile at 10,8 based on EVENT_HOLE_IN_BURNED_TOWER and EVENT_RELEASED_THE_BEASTS), Eusine SCENE_BURNEDTOWER1F_MEET_EUSINE intro cutscene, Rival coord_event at (11,9) triggering SCENE_BURNEDTOWER1F_RIVAL_BATTLE (three team variants by starter choice), post-battle earthquake opening skyfall hole, Morty NPC removed by EVENT_BURNED_TOWER_MORTY, HP Up item ball + hidden Ether + hidden Ultra Ball, smashable rock at (15,4)

Migrate burned_tower_b1f: 20x18-tile basement with three legendary beast pairs (Raikou at 7,3; Entei at 12,3; Suicune at 10,4) using BEASTS_1/BEASTS_2 dual object scheme, coord_event at (10,6) triggering ReleaseTheBeasts cutscene (wakeup/flee animations, sliding movement sequences per beast), Suicune three-phase extended movement, InitRoamMons for Raikou+Entei, cascade setmapscene calls (Ecruteak Gym unlock, Cianwood Suicune chain), Eusine post-scene farewell at (10,12), TM39 Endure item ball, Strength boulder at (17,8), BurnedTowerB1FLadderCallback managing secondary ladder at (6,14)

Verify wild encounter tables: burned_tower_1f 4% rate (Rattata/Koffing/Zubat/Raticate, all time periods identical), burned_tower_b1f 6% rate (Rattata/Koffing/Zubat/Weezing, all identical) — use 'day' table as default if time-of-day not yet implemented
Verify all warp_event coordinates cross-reference with pokecrystal-master ASM: DANCE_THEATER.asm, WISE_TRIOS_ROOM.asm, ECRUTEAK_TIN_TOWER_ENTRANCE.asm, BURNED_TOWER_1F.asm, BURNED_TOWER_B1F.asm
Register shared-template interiors for Ecruteak: ecruteak_pokecenter_1f and ecruteak_mart using PokemonCenter and Mart templates respectively

source_data.dance_theater: 12x14 tiles, mansion tileset. Kimono Girls: Naoko (counterclockwise spin, Flareon L17), Sayo (random fast, Espeon L17), Zuki (random slow, Umbreon L17), Kuni (random fast, Vaporeon L17), Miki (clockwise spin, Jolteon L17). All prize_money 680, line_of_sight 0 (no auto-challenge). Gentleman at (7,10) with Rhydon companion at (6,8). HM03 Surf reward on EVENT_GOT_HM03_SURF when all 5 beaten. Two bg_events (flower panels) at (5,6) and (6,6). Single south exit via exit-mat tiles at (5,13) and (6,13) → ecruteak_city warp 8.
source_data.wise_trios_room: 8x8 tiles, pokecenter tileset. Three-state NPC system via WiseTriosRoomWiseTrioCallback. State 1 (WISE_TRIO_1): observer sages at (6,2), (6,7), (7,5) with lore dialogue. State 2 (WISE_TRIO_2): trainer sages Gaku (4,2, Noctowl+Flareon L32), Masa (4,6, Noctowl+Jolteon L32), Koji (6,4, Noctowl+Vaporeon L32, line_of_sight 2). Sage gatekeeper 1 at (4,6) conditional on EVENT_RANG_CLEAR_BELL_1, sage 2 at (5,6) on EVENT_RANG_CLEAR_BELL_2. Coord events at (4,7) and (5,7) for animated SAGE_BLOCKS scene. Wandering sage at (6,9) and Gramps at (3,11) conditional on EVENT_ECRUTEAK_TIN_TOWER_ENTRANCE_WANDERING_SAGE. Multi-state dialogue per pokecrystal. source_data.burned_tower_1f: 20x18 tiles, TILESET_RUIN. MUSIC_BURNED_TOWER. Eusine (SPRITE_SUPER_NERD) at (12,12) for MEET_EUSINE scene. Rival (SPRITE_RIVAL) at (8,9) triggers via coord (11,9) when SCENE_BURNEDTOWER1F_RIVAL_BATTLE. Rival teams: vs Chikorita-player: Haunter/Magnemite/Zubat/Bayleef; vs Cyndaquil-player: Haunter/Magnemite/Zubat/Quilava; vs Totodile-player: Haunter/Magnemite/Zubat/Croconaw. Coord event (10,6) → ReleaseTheBeasts when SCENE_BURNEDTOWERB1F_RELEASE_THE_BEASTS. Raikou flees DOWN+LEFT; Entei flees RIGHT+DOWN+RIGHT; Suicune has three-phase movement then lingers. ReleaseTheBeasts sets EVENT_RELEASED_THE_BEASTS, calls InitRoamMons for Raikou+Entei, setmapscene ECRUTEAK_GYM SCENE_ECRUTEAKGYM_NOOP, setmapscene CIANWOOD_CITY SCENE_CIANWOODCITY_SUICUNE_AND_EUSINE, removes Morty/Eusine from 1F. unstructured_notes: MIGRATION AREA — these maps were partially built in a previous agentic experiment. Verify tile layout matches pokecrystal-master ASM exactly before assuming old data is correct. The Dance Theater has Kimono Girls accessible to the player for battle only when approached (line_of_sight 0) — do not assign auto-encounter behavior. The Wise Trio's Room uses WiseTriosRoomWiseTrioCallback on MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS every map load to set the correct NPC visibility flags. The Tin Tower Entrance has a sophisticated multi-scene state machine driven by EcruteakTinTowerEntranceInitializeSagesCallback. The Burned Tower hole-and-ladder callback is tile-modifying (MAPCALLBACK_TILES), not object-modifying. The ReleaseTheBeasts script has cascade effects that permanently alter 4 other maps' scenes — this is a critical story progression gate. Rival teams in Burned Tower are TRAINERTYPE_MOVES (explicit move lists), not TRAINERTYPE_NORMAL. BATTLETYPE_SUICUNE is used for the Suicune fight on Tin Tower 1F (later sprint), not here. The changeblock calls in ReleaseTheBeasts use hardcoded tile constants ($32 for floor, $1b for secondary ladder) that must be verified against the tileset.

unstructured_notes: NEW CONTENT — Tin Tower does not exist in the current engine. Verify before starting: (1) is the tin_tower tileset defined in the engine? (2) does the map system support multiple same-tileset floors sharing a music track without resetting? The BATTLETYPE_SUICUNE battle type must already exist from prior sprints covering Suicune mechanics — confirm it is wired correctly before triggering it here. The TinTower1FNPCsCallback is the most complex callback in this area — it has three branches: if Rainbow Wing AND fought Ho-Oh → show Eusine only; if no Rainbow Wing AND EliteFour beaten AND BeastsCheck → clear WISE_TRIO_1, set WISE_TRIO_2 (show trainer sages) and show beasts; otherwise → show beasts filtered by MonCheck. The Suicune encounter on 1F is a stationary battle (not roaming), unique BATTLETYPE_SUICUNE. After the fight EVENT_FOUGHT_SUICUNE clears beasts from all prior encounter sites. The Rare Candy on 5F is the highest-value openly visible item in the lower tower and should be placed precisely at (9,9) to match pokecrystal.

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint covers both migrated maps (Burned Tower, Dance Theater, Tin Tower Entrance, Wise Trio's Room from Group 25) and new content (Tin Tower 1F-5F from Group 26). Migration failures are more likely in Burned Tower since it had complex callback-driven tile modifications. New content failures are more likely in Tin Tower warp connections since the four-to-one 4F→5F warp system is non-standard. The most fragile point is the ReleaseTheBeasts cascade — it touches 4 other maps' scene flags and must set them all atomically before the cutscene ends. If any cascade effect is missing, subsequent story events (Morty gym, Cianwood Suicune chase) will be broken. QA should NOT modify game logic — report all failures as filed issues for coders to fix.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: NEW CONTENT — floors 6F-9F and Roof are entirely absent from the current engine. Verify before starting: (1) the MUSIC_HO_OH_BATTLE track ID is defined; (2) BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM is defined from prior work; (3) reloadmapafterbattle is handled as a post-battle engine call, not a script command that needs separate implementation. The 7F internal warp pair at (8,3)↔(12,7) is unusual — two tiles on the same floor pointing to each other. The engine must handle same-map warp targets without creating infinite loops; typically this works because the warp deposits the player at a DIFFERENT position where stepping off the tile ends the warp state. The secret warp at 7F (6,9)→9F (16,7) is important for speedrunning but also important for game feel — players who explore methodically get a shortcut reward. The Max Elixir at 8F uses the original ASM spelling 'MAX_ELIXER' (typo in Crystal source) — preserve this constant name to match pokecrystal. Ho-Oh at level 60 with BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM is the highest-level forced encounter in Crystal; the FORCEITEM flag interaction with the Rainbow Wing item must be verified. After EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH is set (caught, defeated, or fled), Ho-Oh never respawns — the roof is permanently empty. The unused Ho-Oh/Lugia text strings on 9F should be present in the ASM as unreferenced labels (matching pokecrystal fidelity).

unstructured_notes: MIGRATION AREA — Route 38, Route 39, and the Moo Moo Farm buildings existed in the old engine. Verify all warp coordinates and NPC positions against pokecrystal-master ASM files before assuming old data is canonical. Dana's Thunderstone gift is one of the few gifts exclusively available through the phone rematch system — must use ENGINE_DANA_HAS_THUNDERSTONE flag to prevent double-giving. Derek's phone exchange is conditional on having a Pikachu (checkmon) at time of first defeat — if no Pikachu, he does NOT exchange numbers. Pokefan Jaime uses a custom night-time battle script (faceplayer + checktime NITE) rather than the standard trainer macro; this is distinct from normal trainers and requires the time-of-day check system to be working. Jaime does NOT auto-challenge during day — she idles with 'I hope it gets dark soon' dialogue. Moomoo Milk is the only HP-restoring item available from a shop on Route 39 (500/bottle, restores 100 HP) — the checkitem gate ensures the player cannot stack multiple bottles. The Sailor Eugene trainer references coming back to Olivine ('I just got back to OLIVINE') — flavor text confirming Olivine is immediately south. Note that Route 38 is east-west while Route 39 is primarily north-south with a jog east at the top — the map connection offsets (route_38 east→ecruteak offset -5; route_39 east→route_38 offset 0; route_39 south→olivine offset -5) must be set correctly for map edge stitching.
technical_debt_notes: Much of this map was already built in a previous agentic design experiment. Rather than delete the existing implementation, this sprint should carefully evaluate the current map layout against the new canonical area data (from data/world_areas/*.json). Keep what aligns with the new layout, consider fundamental consequences of layout changes (NPC positions, collision tiles, warp coordinates, connection offsets), but ultimately migrate toward the new map specification. The world should change gradually — by the time all world-building sprints complete, every map should fully match the new canonical data.

goal: QA audit of Tin Tower upper half and Routes 38-39 corridor — verify Groups 28-29: Ho-Oh encounter mechanics, complete 9-floor tower traversal, Moomoo Farm sidequest integrity, and end-to-end Ecruteak-to-Olivine connectivity
abstract_tasks: Verify Tin Tower 6F-9F warp connections: simulate ascending from 5F through 6F→7F→8F→9F→Roof and descending in reverse; confirm all stair/warp tile coordinates match pokecrystal (6F up at 3,9; 7F down at 3,9 up at 10,15; 8F down at 2,5 five-up-warps; 9F seven-warps including roof at 7,9); confirm no softlocks

Verify 7F internal warp puzzle: step on tile (8,3) and confirm arrival at (12,7); step on (12,7) and confirm arrival at (8,3); verify neither tile is the same as the floor it teleports from (i.e., bidirectional without infinite loop); verify player can walk off the arrival tile before the warp re-triggers

Verify 7F secret warp: step on tile (6,9) and confirm arrival on 9F at tile (16,7); verify this warp is otherwise indistinct (same visual tile as floor, no signal); verify that 9F warp_id 5 at (16,7) returns to 7F warp_id 5 correctly (round-trip)

Verify 8F/9F maze: enter 8F from 7F (arrive at 2,5 via warp_id 1); attempt each of the five 8F→9F warps in sequence and record where each lands on 9F; confirm only warp at 9F (7,9) proceeds to Roof, all others return to 8F or 7F; confirm HP Up at 9F (9,1) is accessible from at least one 8F entry point without needing the roof warp

Verify Ho-Oh Roof encounter: simulate three states — (1) arrive on Roof without Rainbow Wing: Ho-Oh absent, only stair visible; (2) arrive with Rainbow Wing and EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH not set: Ho-Oh visible at (9,5), interaction triggers level 60 BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM battle; (3) arrive after catching/defeating/fleeing Ho-Oh: Ho-Oh absent, EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH persists; confirm MUSIC_HO_OH_BATTLE plays on map entry, confirm reloadmapafterbattle fires correctly after battle

Verify all tower items accessible: Full Heal (3F 3,14), Ultra Ball+PP Up+Escape Rope (4F), hidden Max Potion (4F 11,6), Rare Candy (5F 9,9), hidden Full Restore+Carbos (5F), Max Potion (6F 8,8), Max Revive (7F 16,1), Full Restore+Max Elixir+Nugget (8F), HP Up (9F 9,1); none should be duplicatable via map reload

Verify Route 38-39 traversal end-to-end: enter from Ecruteak via gate building, walk Route 38 west to Route 39 connection, walk Route 39 south to Olivine City border; confirm map connections stitch seamlessly (Route 38 east→Ecruteak offset -5; Route 39 east→Route 38 offset 0; Route 39 south→Olivine offset -5); confirm no invisible walls or passability errors at map borders

Verify Moomoo Farm sidequest: talk to Dairy Farmer (EVENT_TALKED_TO_FARMER_ABOUT_MOOMOO set); enter Barn; confirm Berry prompt appears; feed exactly 7 Berries tracking milestone dialogue (3, 5, 7); confirm MUSIC_HEAL plays and EVENT_HEALED_MOOMOO sets on 7th Berry; return to Farmhouse and confirm Milk shop is open (price 500, one-at-a-time limit) and Pokefan F gives TM13 Snore exactly once via EVENT_GOT_TM13_SNORE_FROM_MOOMOO_FARM; verify Moomoo Milk checkitem gate prevents buying a second bottle while holding one

Verify Route 38/39 trainer battles and phone contacts: defeat Lass Dana, confirm phone contact registered, simulate a rematch call and verify team scaling; defeat Pokefan Derek with Pikachu in party, confirm number exchanged; defeat Derek without Pikachu in party (reload save), confirm number NOT exchanged; test Pokefan Jaime: approach at day (no battle), approach at night (battle triggers)

source_data.qa_focus_tin_tower_upper: Critical warp pairs to verify: 7F internal pair (8,3)↔(12,7) warp_ids 3/4; 7F secret (6,9)→9F (16,7) warp_ids 5/5; 8F five-up-warps to 9F: (2,11)→(12,3), (16,7)→(2,5), (10,3)→(12,7), (14,15)→(6,13), (6,9)→(8,13). Only 9F tile (7,9) reaches roof. Max Elixir at 8F uses constant MAX_ELIXER (not MAX_ELIXIR) — verify item ID is correct and matches pokecrystal.
source_data.qa_focus_ho_oh: BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM at level 60. Callback TinTowerRoofHoOhCallback checks: (1) EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH → disappear; (2) else checkitem RAINBOW_WING → appear; (3) else → disappear. After battle EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH must persist across game reloads. MUSIC_HO_OH_BATTLE must play for the roof map, not MUSIC_TIN_TOWER. Confirm reloadmapafterbattle does not create duplicate map-entry callbacks.
source_data.qa_focus_moomoo: wMooMooBerries RAM variable must persist correctly across Barn map exits and reentries during the feeding sequence. Milestone checks at count==3, 5, 7 (not >= checks — the game uses exact equality in pokecrystal). EVENT_TALKED_TO_FARMER_ABOUT_MOOMOO gates the Berry prompt — if not set, Moomoo gives a weak cry but no interaction. TM13 constant name: TM_SNORE. Milk constant: MOOMOO_MILK. Price constant: ROUTE39FARMHOUSE_MILK_PRICE = 500.
source_data.qa_focus_routes: Route 38 connection offsets: east→ecruteak_city offset -5 means ecruteak starts 5 tiles above route_38's top edge. Route 39 south→olivine_city offset -5. Route 39 east→route_38 offset 0 (direct stitch). These offsets must allow player to walk seamlessly between maps without visual jumps. Wild encounter tables: Route 38 and Route 39 both 25%, no water encounters. Farfetch'd absent at night on both routes (night table has no Farfetch'd slot). Snubbull 1% on Route 38 day/morning only.

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint covers entirely new content (Tin Tower 6F-Roof from Group 28) and migrated content (Routes 38-39, Moo Moo Farm from Group 29). The most likely failures in new content: 7F internal warp pair creating a softlock if the engine places the player back on the warp tile after teleport. The most likely failures in migrated content: map connection offsets being wrong (causing map-border visual gaps) and the Moomoo Berry counter not persisting across map loads. The Ho-Oh encounter is a critical one-time legendary — verify that EVENT_FOUGHT_HO_OH cannot be cleared by any mechanic (no respawn under any condition). Also verify that the route end-to-end connectivity is complete: a player starting at the Ecruteak east exit should be able to walk without interruption through gate→Route 38→Route 39→Olivine City entrance after this sprint completes. QA should NOT modify game logic — report all failures as filed issues.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: MIGRATION SPRINT — Groups 31 and 32 together complete the final pokecrystal ASM migration pass. After sprint 32 all remaining world content is net-new implementation. Olivine Gym has an unusual gating mechanic: the gym building is accessible but Jasmine herself is absent until EVENT_JASMINE_RETURNED_TO_GYM is set (she is upstairs in the lighthouse). The gym trainers Ironmask and Ridley can be fought before Jasmine is available. Sailor Huey on 2F is the earliest available phone-rematch trainer encountered in the lighthouse climb — his 4-team system must be implemented using the standard crusty phone rematch architecture. Gentleman Alfred is important as the narrative seed for the Amphy sickness quest chain. HM04 Strength from the Cafe is required for Cianwood Gym — flag it properly so it can only be obtained once.

unstructured_notes: MIGRATION SPRINT — This completes the final group of pokecrystal ASM migration. After this sprint all remaining world content is net-new implementation. The music transition at 6F (MUSIC_VIOLET_CITY vs MUSIC_LIGHTHOUSE on all lower floors) is confirmed in pokecrystal blocks.asm and must be implemented exactly. The SecretPotion cutscene is one of the most complex in the Johto overworld — it has multiple movement scripts, conditional music changes, screen flash effects, and event flag state changes. Implement it as a dedicated cutscene module. Amphy's sick cry uses PlaySlowCry (distorted pitch) — this must work correctly in the crusty audio system. Bird Keeper Theo on 3F and Denis on 5F both use SPRITE_YOUNGSTER despite being Bird Keeper class — this is a known pokecrystal quirk, preserve it exactly. S.S. Aqua port boarding only activates post-game (Mon/Fri schedule) — gate correctly, do not make it accessible during the main quest.

unstructured_notes: NEW CONTENT sprint — no migration, all implementation is net-new. Route 41 is the largest map (50x54 tiles) and has more trainers than any other route. Implement Whirl Island warp entries as stubs that load correct cave interior maps (Whirl Island interiors are a separate sprint). Monica of Monday is a recurring gift NPC — she appears daily (Mondays only) and gives Sharp Beak, then can be called on the phone for more. Chuck's gym boulder puzzle uses Strength (HM04 from Olivine Cafe) — this should already be unlocked if player followed normal progression. Chuck's intro animation (bouncing then boulder throw) is a scripted sequence before battle — implement as a movement script + sound effect. The pharmacy SecretPotion is only available after Jasmine has explained Amphy's sickness (EVENT_JASMINE_EXPLAINED_AMPHYS_SICKNESS) — the pharmacist refuses to sell or give it otherwise. Eusine is a recurring character who tracks Suicune throughout the game — this Cianwood scene is his second appearance (first was Ecruteak City). ENGINE_STORMBADGE enables HM02 Fly outside battle.
technical_debt_notes: This area does not exist in the current engine and is being built fresh. Verify that the map system supports the tile dimensions and features of this new area. If compatibility issues arise, address them as part of this sprint.

unstructured_notes: NEW CONTENT sprint — all implementation is net-new. The ice sliding puzzle in Mahogany Gym requires implementing a physics-like slide mechanic: when the player moves on ice tiles, they continue in that direction until hitting a wall or non-ice tile. This is a non-trivial engine feature — the architect should design it as a reusable TileEffect system since Ice Path (later dungeon) uses the same mechanic. Hiker Benjamin on Route 42 uses SPRITE_POKEFAN_M despite being Hiker class — preserve this pokecrystal quirk. The Suicune scene on Route 42 is Crystal-only (not in Gold/Silver) — implement it behind a Crystal-mode flag if the engine has one, otherwise implement unconditionally. The RageCandyBar merchant is one of the most memorable Mahogany Town details — his blocking position is tile (7,9) in front of gym door. EVENT_CLEARED_ROCKET_HIDEOUT is set in a future sprint (Rocket Hideout) so for now the merchant is permanently blocking; the gate logic should be wired but will only unlock when the hideout sprint completes. Pryce's Piloswine is the strongest Pokemon in the gym and knows Blizzard — do not reduce its level or moves.
technical_debt_notes: This area does not exist in the current engine and is being built fresh. Verify that the map system supports the tile dimensions and features of this new area. If compatibility issues arise, address them as part of this sprint.

unstructured_notes: This QA covers sprints 34 and 35 which are both new content sprints. Key testing priorities: (1) Ice sliding physics in Mahogany Gym — this is the first implementation of ice mechanics in crusty and must be rigorously tested before Ice Path dungeon sprint. (2) SecretPotion gate — the pharmacy conditional must be airtight, as this is a quest-critical item. (3) Route 41 is the largest map — test performance and traversal time. (4) Whirl Island entrances are stub warps that will be completed in a future dungeon sprint — verify they load without crashing but do not need to be fully navigable. (5) Hiker Benjamin SPRITE_POKEFAN_M is a known pokecrystal quirk — QA must not flag it as a bug. (6) The RageCandyBar blocker relies on a future event flag (EVENT_CLEARED_ROCKET_HIDEOUT) — test the blocked state now, and note that the unblocked state cannot be fully tested until the Rocket Hideout sprint completes.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: Route 43 gate Rocket toll: ROUTE43GATE_TOLL constant = 1000. checkmoney/takemoney script commands. Player cannot be stopped indefinitely — always passes after paying whatever they have. The Red Gyarados is guaranteed shiny via BATTLETYPE_FORCESHINY — the game forces shiny regardless of RNG. Red Scale is given after the battle regardless of outcome (win, lose, or run). Lance's teleport uses LanceOfRageLanceTeleportIntoSkyMovement. The Wesley NPC uses SPRITE_SUPER_NERD despite being named Wesley of Wednesday. B1F cameras: GRUNTM_20 then GRUNTM_21 appear sequentially from the camera position and walk toward the player. Warp panel dialogue 'The boulder fell through.' does not apply here — Scientist Jed reveals the warp panel exists after his battle. Trap battles use BATTLETYPE_TRAP which causes the surprise attack animation.
